behaviors.description: The content passed to the summary-generation step includes data retrieved from all three sources.
behaviors.justification: THREE-GATE: (1) Evidence: behavior requires data from "all three sources" to be present in content handed to summary generation. (2) Lower-level miss: a Unit test of only the merge function can pass while an adapter mapping between one source module and the aggregator drops a section key. (3) Boundary type: handoff-state across source clients -> aggregation -> summary input. Rejected higher levels: no behavior text requires real OS/network/subprocess at assertion time (System), real model-output correctness (Agentic), or multiple agentic decisions (Workflow).
behaviors.test_description: Wire real data-collection modules and summary-input assembler with in-process fakes for the three source clients. Return distinct sentinel values from each source, run one report build, and assert the exact payload passed into summary generation contains all three sections with the sentinels intact.

behaviors.description: When one data source is unavailable, the report is produced with a `data unavailable` note for that section rather than aborting.
behaviors.justification: THREE-GATE: (1) Evidence: behavior combines source failure handling and report rendering ("one data source is unavailable" yet report still produced with note). (2) Lower-level miss: Unit tests of error-handler alone miss propagation bugs where collector exceptions are not converted into section-level placeholders before render. (3) Boundary type: handoff-state between collector/orchestrator/renderer. Rejected higher levels: no explicit need for real OS/network effects (System), no dependence on model-output quality (Agentic), no multi-agent sequence (Workflow).
behaviors.test_description: Run report generation with two source modules returning valid data and one source module raising an availability error via in-process stub. Assert generation completes, output includes a `data unavailable` marker only for the failed section, and process does not abort.

behaviors.description: The narrative summary accurately represents the key values present in the input data.
behaviors.justification: THREE-GATE: (1) Evidence: behavior judges semantic accuracy of a "narrative summary" against input key values. (2) Lower-level miss: Integration with stubbed summary output always passes and cannot reveal omission/hallucination/misstated values from the real model. (3) Boundary type: model-output. Rejected higher level Workflow: behavior describes one summary-generation decision, not multiple sequential agentic decisions with inter-step handoff.
behaviors.test_description: Use the real summary-generation model/API on fixed datasets with known key metrics. Evaluate whether generated text preserves those key values (including direction/magnitude) over repeated runs with acceptance thresholds for omission and contradiction rates.

behaviors.description: A complete report — from data collection through email delivery — reaches the configured recipient's inbox correctly.
behaviors.justification: THREE-GATE: (1) Evidence: "complete report ... through email delivery ... reaches ... inbox" requires end-to-end delivery behavior. (2) Lower-level miss: Integration tests with mocked mail transport can pass while SMTP/API auth, TLS, routing, or mailbox delivery semantics fail in real infrastructure. (3) Boundary type: real-infra. Rejected higher level Agentic: behavior asserts delivery-to-inbox correctness, not quality of model decisions; model output content is not the pass/fail target. Rejected Workflow: no explicit multi-agentic chained decisions in the acceptance statement.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the script in a test environment using real internal source endpoints and real email transport to a test inbox for the configured recipient. Assert inbox receipt, correct recipient addressing, and presence of report body/sections expected for that run.

behaviors.description: When the email delivery service is unreachable, the generated report is saved to a local file and delivery is retried on the next scheduled run.
behaviors.justification: THREE-GATE: (1) Evidence: behavior includes "email delivery service is unreachable," persistence to "local file," and retry on "next scheduled run." (2) Lower-level miss: Integration fakes can miss real network failure modes, file-permission/path issues, and cross-run persistence/retry behavior. (3) Boundary type: real-infra (network + filesystem + scheduler/process boundary). Rejected higher levels: no requirement that model-output quality determines pass/fail (Agentic) and no multi-agentic chain (Workflow).
behaviors.test_description: Run the script with real mail endpoint intentionally unreachable. Verify a fallback report file is created at the configured local path. On a subsequent scheduled execution after restoring mail service, verify the saved report is retried and successfully delivered, and fallback state is cleared or marked sent.
